jar.c

00001 /*
00002  * Copyright  1990-2006 Sun Microsystems, Inc. All Rights Reserved.
00003  * DO NOT ALTER OR REMOVE COPYRIGHT NOTICES OR THIS FILE HEADER
00004  *

00006  * modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License version
00007  * 2 only, as published by the Free Software Foundation.
00008  *
00009  * This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but
00010  * WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
00011  * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU
00012  * General Public License version 2 for more details (a copy is
00013  * included at /legal/license.txt).
00014  *
00015  * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
00016  * version 2 along with this work; if not, write to the Free Software
00017  * Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA
00018  * 02110-1301 USA

00020  * Please contact Sun Microsystems, Inc., 4150 Network Circle, Santa
00021  * Clara, CA 95054 or visit www.sun.com if you need additional
00022  * information or have any questions.
00023  *
00024  */
00025 
00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: JAR class loader.
00029  * FILE:      jar.c
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Routines for reading contents of a JAR file. The routines
00031  *            are optimized to reduce code size and run-time memory
00032  *            requirement so that they can run happily on small devices.
00033  *=======================================================================*/
00034 
00035 /*=========================================================================
00036  * Include files
00037  *=======================================================================*/
00038 
00039 #include "wrappers.h"
00040 
00041 #include "oobj.h"
00042 #include "typedefs.h"
00043 #include "jar.h"
00044 #include "jarint.h"
00045 #include "jartables.h"
00046 
00047 /*=========================================================================
00048  * Globals and extern declarations
00049  *=======================================================================*/
00050 
00051 static bool decodeDynamicHuffmanTables(inflaterState *state,
00052                                        HuffmanCodeTable **lcodesPtr,
00053                                        HuffmanCodeTable **dcodesPtr);
00054 
00055 static HuffmanCodeTable *makeCodeTable(inflaterState *state,
00056                        unsigned char *codelen,
00057                        unsigned numElems,
00058                        unsigned maxQuickBits);
00059 
00060 static bool inflateHuffman(inflaterState *state, bool fixedHuffman);
00061 static bool inflateStored(inflaterState *state);
00062 
00063 /*===========================================================================
00064  * FUNCTION:  inflate
00065  * TYPE:      jar file decoding
00066  * OVERVIEW   Used for decoding JAR files given the compressed data source,
00067  *            compressed data length, buffer to store decompressed data and
00068  *            length of decompression buffer.
00069  *
00070  * INTERFACE:
00071  *   parameters: compressed data source, compressed data length,
00072  *               buffer to store decompressed data, decompression buffer size
00073  *   returns:    TRUE if the data was encoded in a supported <method> and the
00074  *               size of the decoded data is exactly the same as <decompLen>
00075  *               FALSE if an error occurs
00076  * NOTE:
00077  *    The caller of this method must insure that this function can safely get
00078  *    up to INFLATER_EXTRA_BYTES beyond compData + compLen without causing
00079  *    any problems.
00080  *    The inflater algorithm occasionally reads one more byte than it needs
00081  *    to.  But it double checks that it doesn't actually care what's in that
00082  *    extra byte.
00083  *===========================================================================*/
00084 
00085 /* Change some definitions so that this compiles nicely, even if it is
00086  * compiled as part of something that requires real malloc() and free()
00087  */
00088 #if !JAR_INFLATER_INSIDE_KVM
00089 #  undef START_TEMPORARY_ROOTS
00090 #  undef END_TEMPORARY_ROOTS
00091 #  undef MAKE_TEMPORARY_ROOT
00092 #  define START_TEMPORARY_ROOTS
00093 #  define END_TEMPORARY_ROOTS
00094 #  define MAKE_TEMPORARY_ROOT(x)
00095 #  define mallocBytes(x) malloc(x)
00096 #  define freeBytes(x)   if (x == NULL) {} else free(x)
00097 #else
00098 #  define freeBytes(x)
00099 #endif
00100 
00101 bool
00102 inflate(JarCompressedType compData, /* compressed data source */
00103     int compLen,        /* length of compressed data */
00104     unsigned char *decompData, /* buffer to store decompressed data */
00105     int decompLen)        /* length of decompression buffer */
00106 {
00107     inflaterState stateStruct;
00108     bool result;
00109 /* Temporarily define state, so that LOAD_IN, LOAD_OUT, etc. macros work */
00110 #define state (&stateStruct)
00111     stateStruct.out = decompData;
00112     stateStruct.outStart = decompData;
00113     stateStruct.outEnd = decompData + decompLen;
00114 
00115     stateStruct.inFile = compData;
00116     stateStruct.inData = 0;
00117     stateStruct.inDataSize = 0;
00118     stateStruct.inRemaining = compLen + INFLATER_EXTRA_BYTES;
00119 
00120 #ifdef JAR_DEBUG_FILE
00121     {
00122     static int length = 0;
00123     if (length == 0) {
00124         struct stat stat_buffer;
00125         stat(JAR_DEBUG_FILE, &stat_buffer);
00126         length = stat_buffer.st_size;;
00127     }
00128     if (length == decompLen) {
00129         FILE *f = fopen(JAR_DEBUG_FILE, "rb");
00130         state->jarDebugBytes = malloc(length);
00131         fseek(f, 0, SEEK_SET);
00132         fread(state->jarDebugBytes, sizeof(char), length, f);
00133         fclose(f);
00134     } else {
00135         state->jarDebugBytes = NULL;
00136     }
00137     }
00138 #endif
00139 
00140     for(;;) {
00141     int type;
00142     DECLARE_IN_VARIABLES
00143 
00144     LOAD_IN;
00145     NEEDBITS(3);
00146     type = NEXTBITS(3);
00147     DUMPBITS(3);
00148     STORE_IN;
00149 
00150     switch (type >> 1) {
00151         default:
00152         case BTYPE_INVALID:
00153         ziperr("Invalid BTYPE");
00154         result = false;
00155         break;
00156 
00157         case BTYPE_NO_COMPRESSION:
00158         result = inflateStored(state);
00159         break;
00160 
00161         case BTYPE_FIXED_HUFFMAN:
00162         result = inflateHuffman(state, true);
00163         break;
00164 
00165         case BTYPE_DYNA_HUFFMAN:
00166         START_TEMPORARY_ROOTS
00167            result = inflateHuffman(state, false);
00168         END_TEMPORARY_ROOTS
00169         break;
00170     }
00171 
00172     if (!result || (type & 1)) {
00173         break;
00174     }
00175     }
00176 
00177     if (state->inRemaining + (state->inDataSize >> 3) != INFLATER_EXTRA_BYTES) {
00178     ziperr("Error on the input bits");
00179     result = false;
00180     } else if (state->out != state->outEnd) {
00181     ziperr("Error on the output bits");
00182     result = false;
00183     }
00184 
00185 #ifdef JAR_DEBUG_FILE
00186     if (state->jarDebugBytes != NULL) {
00187     free(state->jarDebugBytes);
00188     }
00189 #endif
00190 
00191     /* Remove temporary definition of state defined above */
00192 #undef state
00193 
00194     return result;
00195 }
00196 
00197 /*=========================================================================
00198  * FUNCTION:  inflateStored
00199  * TYPE:      Huffman code Decoding helper function
00200  * OVERVIEW:  Used by inflate() for BTYPE_NO_COMPRESSION.
00201  * INTERFACE:
00202  *   parameters: inflaterState: *state
00203  *
00204  *   returns:    boolean type
00205  *=======================================================================*/
00206 static bool
00207 inflateStored(inflaterState *state)
00208 {
00209     DECLARE_IN_VARIABLES
00210     DECLARE_OUT_VARIABLES
00211     unsigned len, nlen;
00212 
00213     LOAD_IN; LOAD_OUT;
00214 
00215     DUMPBITS(inDataSize & 7);    /* move to byte boundary */
00216     NEEDBITS(32)
00217     len = NEXTBITS(16);
00218     DUMPBITS(16);
00219     nlen = NEXTBITS(16);
00220     DUMPBITS(16);
00221 
00222     ASSERT(inDataSize == 0);
00223 
00224     if (len + nlen != 0xFFFF) {
00225     ziperr("Bad length field");
00226     return false;
00227     } else if ((unsigned) inRemaining < len) {
00228     ziperr("Input overflow");
00229     return false;
00230     } else if (out + len > state->outEnd) {
00231     ziperr("Output overflow");
00232     return false;
00233     } else {
00234     if (!COPY_N_BYTES(out, len)) {
00235         ziperr("Bad Read");
00236         return false;
00237     }
00238     inRemaining -= len;
00239     out += len;
00240     }
00241     STORE_IN;
00242     STORE_OUT;
00243     return true;
00244 }
00245 
00246 /*=========================================================================
00247  * FUNCTION:  inflateHuffman
00248  * TYPE:      Huffman code Decoding helper function
00249  * OVERVIEW:  Used by inflate() for BTYPE_FIXED_HUFFMAN and BTYPE_DYNA_HUFFMAN.
00250  * INTERFACE:
00251  *   parameters: inflaterState: *state
00252  *               bool_t: fixedHuffman
00253  *
00254  *   returns:    boolean type
00255  *=======================================================================*/
00256 static bool
00257 inflateHuffman(inflaterState *state, bool fixedHuffman)
00258 {
00259     DECLARE_IN_VARIABLES
00260     DECLARE_OUT_VARIABLES
00261     unsigned char *outStart = state->outStart;
00262     unsigned char *outEnd = state->outEnd;
00263 
00264     bool noerror = false;
00265     unsigned int quickDataSize = 0, quickDistanceSize = 0;
00266     unsigned int code, litxlen;
00267     HuffmanCodeTable *lcodes = NULL, *dcodes = NULL;
00268 
00269     if (!fixedHuffman) {
00270     if (!decodeDynamicHuffmanTables(state, &lcodes, &dcodes)) {
00271         return false;
00272     }
00273     quickDataSize = lcodes->h.quickBits;
00274     quickDistanceSize = dcodes->h.quickBits;
00275     }
00276 
00277     LOAD_IN;
00278     LOAD_OUT;
00279 
00280     for (;;) {
00281     if (inRemaining < 0) {
00282         goto done_loop;
00283     }
00284     NEEDBITS(MAX_BITS + MAX_ZIP_EXTRA_LENGTH_BITS);
00285 
00286     if (fixedHuffman) {
00287         /*   literal (hex)
00288              * 0x100 - 0x117   7   0.0000.00   -  0.0101.11
00289          *     0 -    8f   8   0.0110.000  -  1.0111.111
00290          *   118 -   11f   8   1.1000.000  -  1.1000.111
00291          *    90 -    ff   9   1.1001.0000 -  1.1111.1111
00292          */
00293 
00294         /* Get 9 bits, and reverse them. */
00295         code = NEXTBITS(9);
00296         code = REVERSE_9BITS(code);
00297         if (code <  0x060) {
00298         /* A 7-bit code  */
00299         DUMPBITS(7);
00300         litxlen = 0x100 + (code >> 2);
00301         } else if (code < 0x190) {
00302         DUMPBITS(8);
00303         litxlen = (code >> 1) + ((code < 0x180) ? (0x000 - 0x030)
00304                                     : (0x118 - 0x0c0));
00305         } else {
00306         DUMPBITS(9);
00307         litxlen = 0x90 + code - 0x190;
00308         }
00309     } else {
00310         GET_HUFFMAN_ENTRY(lcodes, quickDataSize, litxlen, done_loop);
00311     }
00312 
00313     if (litxlen <= 255) {
00314         if (out < outEnd) {
00315 #ifdef JAR_DEBUG_FILE
00316         if (state->jarDebugBytes && state->jarDebugBytes[out - outStart] != litxlen) {
00317             ziperr("Dragon single byte");
00318         }
00319 #endif
00320         *out++ = litxlen;
00321         } else {
00322         goto done_loop;
00323         }
00324     } else if (litxlen == 256) {               /* end of block */
00325         noerror = true;
00326         goto done_loop;
00327     } else if (litxlen > 285) {
00328         ziperr("Invalid literal/length");
00329         goto done_loop;
00330     } else {
00331         unsigned int n = litxlen - LITXLEN_BASE;
00332         unsigned int length = ll_length_base[n];
00333         unsigned int moreBits = ll_extra_bits[n];
00334         unsigned int d0, distance;
00335 
00336         /* The NEEDBITS(..) above took care of this */
00337         length += NEXTBITS(moreBits);
00338         DUMPBITS(moreBits);
00339 
00340         NEEDBITS(MAX_BITS);
00341         if (fixedHuffman) {
00342         d0 = REVERSE_5BITS(NEXTBITS(5));
00343         DUMPBITS(5);
00344         } else {
00345         GET_HUFFMAN_ENTRY(dcodes, quickDistanceSize, d0, done_loop);
00346         }
00347 
00348         if (d0 > MAX_ZIP_DISTANCE_CODE) {
00349         ziperr("Bad distance code");
00350         goto done_loop;
00351         }
00352 
00353         NEEDBITS(MAX_ZIP_EXTRA_DISTANCE_BITS)
00354         distance = dist_base[d0];
00355         moreBits = dist_extra_bits[d0];
00356         distance += NEXTBITS(moreBits);
00357         DUMPBITS(moreBits);
00358 
00359         if (out - distance < outStart) {
00360         ziperr("copy underflow");
00361         goto done_loop;
00362         } else if (out + length > outEnd) {
00363         ziperr("Output overflow");
00364         goto done_loop;
00365         } else {
00366         unsigned char *prev = out - distance;
00367         unsigned char *end  = out + length;
00368         while (out != end) {
00369 #ifdef JAR_DEBUG_FILE
00370             if (state->jarDebugBytes
00371             && state->jarDebugBytes[out - outStart] != *prev) {
00372             ziperr("Dragon copy error");
00373             }
00374 #endif
00375             *out++ = *prev++;
00376         }
00377         }
00378     }
00379     }
00380 
00381  done_loop:
00382     STORE_IN;
00383     STORE_OUT;
00384 
00385     if (!JAR_INFLATER_INSIDE_KVM && !fixedHuffman) {
00386     freeBytes(lcodes);
00387     freeBytes(dcodes);
00388     }
00389     return noerror;
00390 }
00391 
00392 /*=========================================================================
00393  * FUNCTION:  decodeDynamicHuffmanTables
00394  * TYPE:      Huffman code Decoding
00395  * OVERVIEW:  Used by inflateHuffman() for decoding dynamic Huffman tables.
00396  * INTERFACE:
00397  *   parameters: inflaterState: *state
00398  *               HuffmanCodeTable: **lcodesPtr
00399  *               HuffmanCodeTable: **dcodesPtr
00400  *
00401  *   returns:    TRUE if successful in decoding or
00402  *               FALSE if an error occurs
00403  *=======================================================================*/
00404 
00405 static bool
00406 decodeDynamicHuffmanTables(inflaterState *state,
00407                HuffmanCodeTable **lcodesPtr,
00408                HuffmanCodeTable **dcodesPtr) {
00409     DECLARE_IN_VARIABLES
00410 
00411     HuffmanCodeTable *ccodes = NULL;
00412     HuffmanCodeTable *lcodes = NULL;
00413     HuffmanCodeTable *dcodes = NULL;
00414 
00415     int hlit, hdist, hclen;
00416     int i;
00417     unsigned int quickBits;
00418     unsigned char codelen[286 + 32];
00419     unsigned char *codePtr, *endCodePtr;
00420 
00421     LOAD_IN;
00422 
00423     /* 5 Bits: HLIT, # of Literal/Length codes - 257 (257 - 286) */
00424     /* 5 Bits: HDIST, # of Distance codes - 1        (1 - 32) */
00425     /* 4 Bits: HCLEN, # of Code Length codes - 4     (4 - 19) */
00426     NEEDBITS(14);
00427     hlit = 257 + NEXTBITS(5);
00428     DUMPBITS(5);
00429     hdist = 1 + NEXTBITS(5);
00430     DUMPBITS(5);
00431     hclen = 4 + NEXTBITS(4);
00432     DUMPBITS(4);
00433 
00434     /*
00435      * (HCLEN + 4) x 3 bits: code lengths for the code length
00436      *  alphabet given just above, in the order: 16, 17, 18,
00437      *  0, 8, 7, 9, 6, 10, 5, 11, 4, 12, 3, 13, 2, 14, 1, 15
00438      *
00439      *  These code lengths are interpreted as 3-bit integers
00440      *  (0-7); as above, a code length of 0 means the
00441      *  corresponding symbol (literal/length or distance code
00442      *  length) is not used.
00443      */
00444     memset(codelen, 0x0, 19);
00445     for (i=0; i<hclen; i++) {
00446     NEEDBITS(3);        /* 3, plus 7 below */
00447     if (inRemaining < 0) {
00448         goto error;
00449     }
00450     codelen[(int)ccode_idx[i]] = (unsigned char) NEXTBITS(3);
00451     DUMPBITS(3);
00452     }
00453 
00454     ccodes = makeCodeTable(state, codelen, 19, MAX_QUICK_CXD);
00455     if (ccodes == NULL) {
00456     goto error;
00457     }
00458     quickBits = ccodes->h.quickBits;
00459 
00460     /*
00461      * HLIT + 257 code lengths for the literal/length alphabet,
00462      * encoded using the code length Huffman code.
00463      *
00464      * HDIST + 1 code lengths for the distance alphabet,
00465      * encoded using the code length Huffman code.
00466      *
00467      * The code length repeat codes can cross from HLIT + 257 to the
00468      * HDIST + 1 code lengths.  In other words, all code lengths form
00469      * a single sequence of HLIT + HDIST + 258 values.
00470      */
00471 
00472     memset(codelen, 0x0, sizeof(codelen));
00473     for (   codePtr = codelen, endCodePtr = codePtr + hlit + hdist;
00474         codePtr < endCodePtr; ) {
00475     int val;
00476 
00477     if (inRemaining < 0) {
00478         goto error;
00479     }
00480 
00481     NEEDBITS(MAX_BITS + 7);    /* 7 is max repeat bits below */
00482     GET_HUFFMAN_ENTRY(ccodes, quickBits, val, error);
00483 
00484     /*
00485      *  0 - 15: Represent code lengths of 0 - 15
00486      *      16: Copy the previous code length 3 - 6 times.
00487          *          3 + (2 bits of length)
00488      *      17: Repeat a code length of 0 for 3 - 10 times.
00489      *          3 + (3 bits of length)
00490      *      18: Repeat a code length of 0 for 11 - 138 times
00491      *          11 + (7 bits of length)
00492      */
00493     if (val <= 15) {
00494         *codePtr++ = val;
00495     } else if (val <= 18) {
00496         unsigned repeat  = (val == 18) ? 11 : 3;
00497         unsigned bits    = (val == 18) ? 7 : (val - 14);
00498 
00499         repeat += NEXTBITS(bits); /* The NEEDBITS is above */
00500         DUMPBITS(bits);
00501 
00502         if (codePtr + repeat > endCodePtr) {
00503         ziperr("Bad repeat code");
00504         }
00505 
00506         if (val == 16) {
00507         if (codePtr == codelen) {
00508             ziperr("Bad repeat code");
00509             goto error;
00510         }
00511         memset(codePtr, codePtr[-1], repeat);
00512         } else {
00513         /* The values have already been set to zero, above, so
00514          * we don't have to do anything */
00515         }
00516         codePtr += repeat;
00517     } else {
00518         ziperr("Bad code-length code");
00519         goto error;
00520     }
00521     }
00522 
00523     lcodes = makeCodeTable(state, codelen, hlit, MAX_QUICK_LXL);
00524     if (lcodes == NULL) {
00525         goto error;
00526     }
00527 
00528     dcodes = makeCodeTable(state, codelen + hlit, hdist, MAX_QUICK_CXD);
00529     if (dcodes == NULL) {
00530         goto error;
00531     }
00532 
00533     *lcodesPtr = lcodes;
00534     *dcodesPtr = dcodes;
00535     STORE_IN;
00536     if (!JAR_INFLATER_INSIDE_KVM) {
00537     freeBytes(ccodes);
00538     }
00539     return true;
00540 
00541 error:
00542     if (!JAR_INFLATER_INSIDE_KVM) {
00543     freeBytes(ccodes);
00544     freeBytes(dcodes);
00545     freeBytes(lcodes);
00546     }
00547     return false;
00548 }
00549 
00550 /*=========================================================================
00551  * FUNCTION:  makeCodeTable
00552  * TYPE:      Huffman code table creation
00553  * INTERFACE:
00554  *   parameters: inflaterState
00555  *               code length
00556  *               number of elements of the alphabet
00557  *               maxQuickBits
00558  *   returns:    Huffman code table created if successful or
00559  *               NULL if an error occurs
00560  *=======================================================================*/
00561 
00562 HuffmanCodeTable * makeCodeTable(
00563     inflaterState *state,
00564         unsigned char *codelen,    /* Code lengths */
00565         unsigned numElems,      /* Number of elements of the alphabet */
00566         unsigned maxQuickBits)  /* If the length of a code is longer than
00567                                  * <maxQuickBits> number of bits, the code is
00568                                  * stored in the sequential lookup table
00569                                  * instead of the quick lookup array. */
00570 {
00571     unsigned int bitLengthCount[MAX_BITS + 1];
00572     unsigned int codes[MAX_BITS + 1];
00573     unsigned bits, minCodeLen = 0, maxCodeLen = 0;
00574     const unsigned char *endCodeLen = codelen + numElems;
00575     unsigned int code, quickMask;
00576     unsigned char *p;
00577 
00578     HuffmanCodeTable * table;
00579     int mainTableLength, longTableLength, numLongTables;
00580     int tableSize;
00581     int j;
00582 
00583     unsigned short *nextLongTable;
00584 
00585     /* Count the number of codes for each code length */
00586     memset(bitLengthCount, 0, sizeof(bitLengthCount));
00587     for (p = codelen; p < endCodeLen; p++) {
00588     bitLengthCount[*p]++;
00589     }
00590 
00591     if (bitLengthCount[0] == numElems) {
00592     ziperr("Bad code table -- empty");
00593         return NULL;
00594     }
00595 
00596     /* Find the minimum and maximum.  It's faster to do it in a separate
00597      * loop that goes 1..MAX_BITS, than in the above loop that looks at
00598      * every code element */
00599     code = minCodeLen = maxCodeLen = 0;
00600     for (bits = 1; bits <= MAX_BITS; bits++) {
00601     codes[bits] = code;
00602     if (bitLengthCount[bits] != 0) {
00603         if (minCodeLen == 0) minCodeLen = bits;
00604         maxCodeLen = bits;
00605         code += bitLengthCount[bits] << (MAX_BITS - bits);
00606     }
00607     }
00608 
00609     if (INCLUDEDEBUGCODE) {
00610     if (code != (1 << MAX_BITS)) {
00611         code += (1 << (MAX_BITS - maxCodeLen));
00612         if (code != (1 << MAX_BITS)) {
00613         ziperr("Unexpected bit codes");
00614         }
00615     }
00616     }
00617 
00618     /* Calculate the size of the code table and allocate it. */
00619     if (maxCodeLen <= maxQuickBits) {
00620     /* We don't need any subtables.  We may even be able to get
00621      * away with a table smaller than maxCodeLen
00622      */
00623     maxQuickBits = maxCodeLen;
00624     mainTableLength = (1 << maxCodeLen);
00625     numLongTables = longTableLength = 0;
00626     } else {
00627     mainTableLength = (1 << maxQuickBits);
00628     numLongTables = (1 << MAX_BITS) - codes[maxQuickBits + 1];
00629     numLongTables = numLongTables >> (MAX_BITS - maxQuickBits);
00630     longTableLength = 1 << (maxCodeLen - maxQuickBits);
00631     }
00632     ASSERT(mainTableLength == 1 << maxQuickBits);
00633     tableSize = sizeof(HuffmanCodeTableHeader)
00634           + (mainTableLength + numLongTables * longTableLength) *
00635                sizeof(table->entries[0]);
00636     table = (HuffmanCodeTable*)mallocBytes(tableSize);
00637     nextLongTable = &table->entries[mainTableLength];
00638     MAKE_TEMPORARY_ROOT(table);
00639 
00640     memset(table, 0, tableSize);
00641 
00642     table->h.maxCodeLen   = maxCodeLen;
00643     table->h.quickBits    = maxQuickBits;
00644 
00645     quickMask = (1 << maxQuickBits) - 1;
00646 
00647     for (p = codelen; p < endCodeLen; p++) {
00648     unsigned short huff;
00649     bits = *p;
00650     if (bits == 0) {
00651         continue;
00652     }
00653     /* Get the next code of the current length */
00654     code = codes[bits];
00655     codes[bits] += 1 << (MAX_BITS - bits);
00656     code = REVERSE_15BITS(code);
00657     huff = ((p - codelen) << 4) + bits;
00658     if (bits <= maxQuickBits) {
00659         unsigned stride = 1 << bits;
00660         for (j = code; j < mainTableLength; j += stride) {
00661         table->entries[j] = huff;
00662         }
00663     } else {
00664         unsigned short *thisLongTable;
00665         unsigned stride = 1 << (bits - maxQuickBits);
00666         unsigned int prefixCode = code & quickMask;
00667         unsigned int suffixCode = code >> maxQuickBits;
00668         if (table->entries[prefixCode] == 0) {
00669         /* This in the first long code with the indicated prefix.
00670          * Create a pointer to the subtable */
00671         long delta = (char *)nextLongTable - (char *)table;
00672         table->entries[prefixCode] = (unsigned short)(HUFFINFO_LONG_MASK | delta);
00673         thisLongTable = nextLongTable;
00674         nextLongTable += longTableLength;
00675         } else {
00676         long delta = table->entries[prefixCode] & ~HUFFINFO_LONG_MASK;
00677         thisLongTable = (unsigned short *)((char *)table + delta);
00678         }
00679         for (j = suffixCode; j < longTableLength; j += stride) {
00680         thisLongTable[j] = huff;
00681         }
00682     }
00683     if (INCLUDEDEBUGCODE && inflateVerbose > 0) {
00684         putchar(' ');
00685 
00686         for (j = 15; j >= 0; j--) {
00687         char c = (j >= (signed)bits) ? ' '
00688             : (code & (1 << j)) ? '1' : '0';
00689         putchar(c);
00690         }
00691         fprintf(stdout,
00692             "   Char = %02zx, Code = %4x\n", (size_t) (p - codelen), code);
00693     }
00694 
00695     }
00696     ASSERT(nextLongTable == &table->entries[mainTableLength + numLongTables * longTableLength]);
00697 
00698     return table;
00699 }
00700 
00701 #if INCLUDEDEBUGCODE
00702 
00703 /*=========================================================================
00704  * FUNCTION:  ziperr
00705  * TYPE:      zip error processing function
00706  * OVERVIEW:  Used by inflate() and other functions for zip error processing.
00707  * INTERFACE:
00708  *   parameters: const char *message
00709  *
00710  *   returns:    nothing
00711  *=======================================================================*/
00712 static void
00713 ziperr(const char *message) {
00714     fprintf(stderr, "Zip Error: %s\n", message);
00715 }
00716 
00717 #endif
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:28 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
